mission.title: Install and Configure Argo CD Operator
mission.description: The Argo CD Operator simplifies the management of Argo CD instances on Kubernetes, enabling GitOps workflows. It is ideal for teams looking to automate their continuous delivery processes using Git as the source of truth.

steps.title: Install Argo CD Operator
steps.description:
Create the necessary resources for the Argo CD Operator. First, apply the CatalogSource, OperatorGroup, and Subscription YAML files to your cluster:

```bash
kubectl apply -f catalog_source.yaml
kubectl apply -f operator_group.yaml
kubectl apply -f subscription.yaml
```

Ensure you are using the correct version of the operator by checking the image in the CatalogSource. For v0.12, use the image `quay.io/argoprojlabs/argocd-operator-registry@sha256:f0d2bb73e8b9d0561c931b2f2afc81cf28ca5711c476b02ac1887770e799ab92`.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description:
Check if the Argo CD Operator is running correctly:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n olm
kubectl get subscriptions -n olm
kubectl get catalogsources -n olm
```

You should see the operator pod in a Running state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
After installation, configure RBAC for Argo CD. Create a role and role binding:

```yaml
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: argocd
  name: argocd-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ['*']
  resources: ['*']
  verbs: ['get', 'list', 'watch', 'create', 'update', 'delete']
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: argocd-role-binding
  namespace: argocd
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: argocd-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: argocd
```

Apply the configuration:
```bash
kubectl apply -f rbac.yaml
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Argo CD Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the Argo CD Operator, delete the Subscription, OperatorGroup, and CatalogSource:

```bash
kubectl delete subscription argocd-operator -n olm
kubectl delete operatorgroup argocd-operator -n olm
kubectl delete catalogsource argocd-catalog -n olm
```

Additionally, clean up any Argo CD instances created by the operator:
```bash
kubectl delete argocd <argocd-instance-name> -n <namespace>
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Argo CD Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the Argo CD Operator to a newer version, first ensure you have a backup of your existing Argo CD configurations. Then, update the Subscription YAML to point to the new version:

1. Edit the `subscription.yaml` to change the `name` field to the new version, e.g., `argocd-operator.v0.12.0`.
2. Apply the updated Subscription:
```bash
kubectl apply -f subscription.yaml
```
3. Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n olm
```

If you encounter issues, you can roll back by reverting the Subscription YAML to the previous version and reapplying it.

troubleshooting.title: Operator Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Argo CD Operator pod is not starting, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <operator-pod-name> -n olm
```
Common issues may include insufficient permissions or missing CRDs. Ensure that all required CRDs are installed.

troubleshooting.title: Argo CD Instance Not Responding
troubleshooting.description:
If your Argo CD instance is not responding, check the status of the Argo CD services:
```bash
kubectl get svc -n argocd
```
Ensure that the services are up and running. If not, check the logs of the Argo CD pods for any errors.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission errors, verify your RBAC configuration. Check the role and role binding:
```bash
kubectl get role -n argocd
kubectl get rolebinding -n argocd
```
Ensure that the service account used by the Argo CD Operator has the necessary permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for multi-cluster deployments and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old RBAC model in favor of a more flexible policy model.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for custom resource definitions (CRDs) for better management of Argo CD resources.

resolution.summary: A successful setup of the Argo CD Operator will have the operator running in the `olm` namespace, with all required resources deployed. You should be able to create and manage Argo CD instances seamlessly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f catalog_source.yaml
kubectl apply -f operator_group.yaml
kubectl apply -f subscription.yaml

kubectl get pods -n olm
kubectl get subscriptions -n olm
kubectl get catalogsources -n olm

apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  namespace: argocd
  name: argocd-role
rules:
- apiGroups: ['*']
  resources: ['*']
  verbs: ['get', 'list', 'watch', 'create', 'update', 'delete']
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: argocd-role-binding
  namespace: argocd
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: Role
  name: argocd-role
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: default
  namespace: argocd

prerequisites.cloudCLI: optional cloud CLI tool (gcloud, aws, az, oci)
prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary permissions to deploy operators.
